lede: A look at September 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Roald Dahl and Daisuke Matsuzaka, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Delhi
events.desc: Delhi, India, is hit by a series of bomb blasts, resulting in 30 deaths and 130 injuries.

events.title: Hurricane Gilbert
events.desc: Hurricane Gilbert is the strongest recorded hurricane in the Western Hemisphere, later replaced by Hurricane Wilma in 2005 (based on barometric pressure).

events.title: Super Mario Bros.
events.desc: Super Mario Bros. is released in Japan for the NES, which starts the Super Mario series of platforming games.

events.title: Mao Zedong
events.desc: Chairman Mao Zedong's second in command and successor Marshal Lin Biao flees China after the failure of an alleged coup. His plane crashes in Mongolia, killing all aboard.

events.title: James Meredith
events.desc: An appeals court orders the University of Mississippi to admit James Meredith, the first African-American student admitted to the segregated university.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Second day of the Battle of Edson's Ridge in the Guadalcanal Campaign. U.S. Marines successfully defeat attacks by the Japanese with heavy losses for the Japanese forces.

events.title: Phineas Gage
events.desc: Vermont railroad worker Phineas Gage survives an iron rod 1+1⁄4 inches (3.2 cm) in diameter being driven through his brain; the reported effects on his behavior and personality stimulate discussion of the nature of the brain and its functions.

events.title: Baltimore
events.desc: In a turning point in the War of 1812, the British fail to capture Baltimore. During the battle, Francis Scott Key composes his poem "Defence of Fort McHenry", which is later set to music and becomes the United States' national anthem.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: A supply wagon sent to relieve Fort Harrison is ambushed in the Attack at the Narrows.

events.title: Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus
events.desc: The Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus on Rome's Capitoline Hill is dedicated on the ides of September.

births.name: Óscar Arias
births.line: Costa Rican politician, President of Costa Rica, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Robert Robinson (chemist)
births.line: English chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Włodzimierz Cimoszewicz
births.line: Polish lawyer and politician, 8th Prime Minister of Poland

deaths.text: Tupac Shakur, American rapper, producer, and actor

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Cross (Assyrian Church of the East)
